ambiguities.original_text: The system should handle many concurrent users
ambiguities.impact: Cannot determine system capacity requirements, scaling needs, or performance targets
questions: How many concurrent users should the system support? (e.g., 100? 1,000? 10,000?)
What is the expected peak load?
What happens when user count exceeds capacity?
ambiguities.suggested_clarification: The system must support at least [NUMBER] concurrent users with [RESPONSE_TIME] response time
examples: The system must support at least 1,000 concurrent users with <2s response time
The system must support 10,000 concurrent users during peak hours (9am-5pm)

ambiguities.original_text: Users should be able to upload files
ambiguities.issue: Missing file type, size limits, error handling, and storage details
ambiguities.impact: Cannot implement without knowing constraints, validation rules, and error scenarios
questions: What file types are allowed? (PDF, images, documents?)
What is the maximum file size?
Where should files be stored? (local, S3, etc.)
What error messages for invalid files?
ambiguities.suggested_clarification: Users must be able to upload [FILE_TYPES] files up to [SIZE_LIMIT]. Files are stored in [STORAGE_LOCATION]. Display error '[ERROR_MESSAGE]' if [ERROR_CONDITION]
examples: Users must be able to upload PDF, DOCX, and image files (JPG, PNG) up to 10MB. Files are stored in AWS S3. Display error 'File too large' if >10MB, 'Invalid file type' if not allowed type.

recommendations.action: Address all critical severity ambiguities before development begins
Clarify high severity ambiguities to prevent rework
